popularStory.headline: Harold Godwinson (c.?1022-14 October 1066)
popularStory.description: Harold Godwinson (c.?1022-14 October 1066), often called Harold II, was the last Anglo-Saxon king of England. Harold reigned from 6 January 1066 until his death at the Battle of Hastings on 14 October …

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当King Harold II of England于1020年出生在維可时，他的父亲Godwin Wulfnothson19岁，母亲Gytha Thorkelsdóttir24岁。他与Eadgyth结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1066年10月14日在英国英格兰素可芭特爾享年46岁，埋葬在英国艾可沃爾坦 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Harold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from here ‘army’ + weald ‘ruler’. In pre-Conquest England, this was reinforced by the related Old Norse name Haraldr, introduced by Scandinavian settlers. The name was not at all popular in England after the Conquest, probably because of its association with the unfortunate King Harold , killed at the Battle of Hastings in 1066 . It was used in some parts of Nottinghamshire in the 16th and 17th centuries, and revived more generally, along with a number of other Old English names, in the 19th century, when it suddenly became extremely popular.

historicEvents.title: England Divided
historicEvents.description: King Canute died in 1035 and England was divided between his three sons. His son Sweyn received Norway, Harthacanute received Denmark, and Harold Harefoot received North England.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

en.lifeSummary: When King Harold II of England was born in 1020, in Wessex, his father, Godwin Wulfnothson, was 19 and his mother, Gytha Thorkelsdóttir, was 24. He married Eadgyth. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 14 October 1066, in Battle, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46, and was buried in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque King Harold II of England est né en 1020, à Wessex, son père, Godwin Wulfnothson, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Gytha Thorkelsdóttir, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Eadgyth. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 14 octobre 1066, à Battle, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Waltham Abbey, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als King Harold II of England 1020 in Wessex geboren wurde, war sein Vater Godwin Wulfnothson 19 und seine Mutter Gytha Thorkelsdóttir 24. Er hat Eadgyth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 14. Oktober 1066 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Battle, Sussex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando King Harold II of England è nato il nel 1020 nel Wessex, il suo padre, Godwin Wulfnothson, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Gytha Thorkelsdóttir, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Eadgyth. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 14 ottobre 1066, nel Battle, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Waltham Abbey, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: King Harold II of Englandが1020年にウエセックスで生まれた時，父Godwin Wulfnothsonは19歳，母Gytha Thorkelsdóttirは24歳であった。Eadgythと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1066年10月14日にイギリスイギリススセックスバットルで46歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスワルサム アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: King Harold II of England(이)가 1020년 웨세스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Godwin Wulfnothson(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Gytha Thorkelsdóttir(은)는 24세였다. 그는 Eadgyth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1066년 10월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 바틀에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 월섬 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.from 6 January 1066 to 14 October 1066: de 6 de janeiro de 1066 a 14 de outubro de 1066
pt.lifeSummary: Quando King Harold II of England nasceu em 1020, em Wessex, seu pai, Godwin Wulfnothson, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Gytha Thorkelsdóttir, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Eadgyth. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de outubro de 1066, em Battle, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда King Harold II of England родился в 1020 г. в местности вессекс, его отцу по имени Godwin Wulfnothson было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Gytha Thorkelsdóttir, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Eadgyth. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 14 октября 1066 г. в местности баттл, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности валтам эбби, эссекс, Англия.

Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 14 de octubre de 1066, en Battle, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultado en Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra.
